questions.question: What is the quickest way to create an inline gallery on any Notion page?

options.text: Click the "+" button next to the page title and select Gallery
options.explanation: The "+" button creates a new block, not an inline gallery view.

options.text: Type "/Gallery view" on the page and press Enter
options.explanation: Typing "/Gallery view" inserts an inline gallery block directly on the page.

options.text: Open the sidebar, drag a full‑page gallery into the page
options.explanation: Dragging a full‑page gallery creates a sub‑page, not an inline gallery.

options.text: Select "Gallery" from the top‑right Layout menu
options.explanation: The Layout menu changes view settings, it doesn’t create a new inline gallery.

questions.question: Which menu do you open to change the card preview type in a gallery?

options.text: Layout
options.explanation: Card preview options (Page cover, Page content, Files & media) are found under Layout.

options.text: Property visibility
options.explanation: Property visibility controls which properties are shown, not the preview type.

options.text: Share
options.explanation: The Share menu deals with permissions and links, not card appearance.

options.text: Settings
options.explanation: There is no generic Settings menu for card preview; it’s specifically under Layout.

questions.question: To hide the name of a card so that only the image shows, which toggle should you switch off?

options.text: Title
options.explanation: There is no separate Title toggle for gallery cards; the relevant toggle is called Name.

options.text: Name
options.explanation: Turning off the Name property hides the card’s title, leaving only the image visible.

options.text: Caption
options.explanation: Caption isn’t a property in gallery view; hiding it won’t affect the card name.

options.text: Description
options.explanation: Description isn’t a toggle in the Properties menu for galleries.

questions.question: What happens to a card’s image when the "Fit image" toggle is turned off?

options.text: The image is cropped to fill the entire card frame
options.explanation: With Fit image off, Notion crops the image so it completely covers the card area.

options.text: The image shrinks proportionally to fit inside the card
options.explanation: That behavior describes Fit image turned on, not off.

options.text: The image is hidden entirely
options.explanation: Turning off Fit image does not hide the image; it only changes how it fills the frame.

options.text: The image is displayed with a scroll bar
options.explanation: Gallery cards never show scrollbars; they either fit or crop the image.

questions.question: How can you change the order in which properties appear on cards in a gallery view?

options.text: Drag the properties using the ⋮⋮ handle in the Property visibility menu
options.explanation: The ⋮⋮ handle lets you reorder properties, affecting their display order on cards.

options.text: Click the arrow keys next to each property name
options.explanation: Arrow keys aren’t provided for reordering properties in Notion.

options.text: Edit the database schema JSON directly
options.explanation: Notion doesn’t expose a JSON schema for end‑users to edit.

options.text: Apply a filter that sorts by the property name
options.explanation: Filters affect which items are shown, not the visual order of property fields on a card.

questions.question: Which two steps are required to turn a full‑page database into an inline database?

options.text: Drag the database into another page, then click the ⋮⋮ button on the sub‑page and select "Turn into inline"
options.explanation: First you move the full‑page database as a sub‑page, then use the context menu to convert it to inline.

options.text: Open the database, click Layout → Card size → Inline, then rename the page
options.explanation: Card size does not change a database’s page type.

options.text: Select the database block, press Shift + I, then confirm the dialog
options.explanation: There is no shortcut like Shift + I for this conversion.

options.text: Duplicate the database, delete the original, and rename the duplicate
options.explanation: Duplicating creates a new database but doesn’t change the original’s page type.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a suggested use case for gallery views?

options.text: Recipe collection
options.explanation: Recipe collection is mentioned as a gallery example.

options.text: Cafe directory
options.explanation: Cafe directory is listed among gallery use cases.

options.text: Board game tracker
options.explanation: Board game tracker is also highlighted as a gallery example.

options.text: Financial spreadsheet
options.explanation: Financial spreadsheet is not included in the article’s gallery suggestions.

questions.question: To make database pages open in full‑screen by default, which setting must you modify?

options.text: Layout → Open pages in → Full page
options.explanation: The article specifies this exact path to set pages to open full‑page.

options.text: Layout → Card size → Large
options.explanation: Card size only changes the thumbnail size, not page opening behavior.

options.text: Property visibility → Show all
options.explanation: Property visibility controls which fields appear, not page display mode.

options.text: Share → Public link → Full screen
options.explanation: The Share menu does not affect how pages open within Notion.

questions.question: What is the main difference between selecting "Page cover" and "Page content" as the card preview option?

options.text: "Page cover" shows the image set as the page’s cover, while "Page content" shows the first image found in the page’s body; "Page cover" is best for uniform thumbnails, "Page content" works when pages don’t have a cover.
options.explanation: Correct: "Page cover" pulls the designated cover image, giving consistent visuals. "Page content" extracts the first inline image, useful when covers aren’t set.

options.text: "Page cover" displays the page title, while "Page content" hides all text; both show the same image.
options.explanation: Incorrect: Neither option displays the title; they both affect image preview, not text.

options.text: "Page cover" only works for databases with a Files & media property, while "Page content" works for any page.
options.explanation: Wrong: "Page cover" works with any page that has a cover image; Files & media is a separate option.

options.text: "Page cover" crops the image, while "Page content" always fits the image; the choice depends on image size.
options.explanation: Incorrect: Cropping vs. fitting is controlled by the Fit image toggle, not by the preview type.

questions.question: If you want the entire image to be visible without cropping and also be able to reposition it within the card, which combination of settings should you use?

options.text: Turn "Fit image" on, then use the "Reposition" handle to move the image
options.explanation: When Fit image is on, the whole image fits inside the frame, and Reposition lets you adjust its placement.

options.text: Turn "Fit image" off, then use the "Reposition" handle
options.explanation: With Fit image off the image is cropped; repositioning won’t reveal hidden parts.

options.text: Leave "Fit image" off and do not use "Reposition"
options.explanation: This results in a cropped image with no ability to adjust its view.

options.text: Turn "Fit image" on and do not use "Reposition"
options.explanation: The image will fit, but you lose the ability to fine‑tune its position.
